message:
test(core): add TRACK_S fast-path register INSERT rusqlite oracle tests

Two new regression tests under `fsqlite-core/tests/fast_path_separation.rs`,
tracked under the `track_s` beads series, verify that the VDBE
register/fast-path INSERT lanes produce byte-identical output to
the rusqlite reference.

## test_track_s_insert_10k_matches_rusqlite_oracle

Opens an in-memory `Connection` and a rusqlite `Connection::open_in_memory()`
in parallel, creates an identical three-column schema
(`id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, label TEXT NOT NULL, score INTEGER NOT NULL`),
and walks 10 000 bound INSERTs through both engines. The insert
statement is deliberately shaped as
`INSERT INTO bench VALUES (?1, lower(?2), abs(?3))` rather than
the trivial `VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)` form so the VDBE cannot short-
circuit to the direct-column direct-append specialization — the
scalar function calls (`lower`, `abs`) keep the path on the
register/VDBE hot path, which is what the TRACK_S contract cares
about.

The test then runs a `SELECT id, label, score FROM bench` against
both engines, sorts both result sets via the existing
`sorted_frank_rows` / `sorted_rusqlite_rows` helpers, asserts
`frank_rows.len() == ROW_COUNT`, and then asserts row-by-row
equality — this catches any drift on the register-path
serialization (e.g. label case normalization, score sign
computation, or rowid allocation) without relying on the ordering
of the INSERT itself.

Both connections run inside a single `BEGIN`/`COMMIT` transaction
and the fsqlite side binds SqliteValue variants directly via
`execute_with_params`, avoiding the string-parsing SQL path.

## test_track_s_select_after_insert_matches_rusqlite_oracle

Smaller lightweight companion that exercises the post-insert
SELECT path: insert a single three-column row into both engines
and then query it back by primary key via
`SELECT label, score FROM t WHERE id = ?1`. Asserts that the
fsqlite row decoded from the register path equals the rusqlite
row bit-for-bit, which locks in the readback side of the fast
path alongside the larger 10K oracle on the write side.

Both tests rely on the existing `rusqlite` dev-dependency on
`fsqlite-core` and the existing `sorted_frank_rows` /
`sorted_rusqlite_rows` helpers that were introduced with earlier
TRACK_ regression suites in this file.
